prompt: Create a vertical watercolor portrait illustration of a graceful woman in profile from the shoulders up, facing right, with the face area intentionally covered by a centered opaque square censor block. The woman has voluminous deep burgundy red hair swept into a loose updo with expressive ink-brush strands and curls around the head and nape. Show a slender neck, bare shoulder, and a delicate dangling earring shaped like a small flower with a teardrop pendant. In the lower left foreground, her hand holds exactly 1 large lily flower with 6 visible pale pink petals, dark stamens, and 3 visible leaves on the stem. Use a limited palette of wine red, dusty rose, pale pink, cream, and dark maroon ink. The censor block should be exactly 1 large square, centered over the face and upper torso area, filled with a muted mauve-to-rose gradient, no text. Background is off-white textured paper with a subtle dotted halftone/fabric grain. Style should feel like loose Japanese fashion watercolor mixed with expressive ink wash: visible brush strokes, splatters, translucent washes, rough outlines, elegant negative space. No typography, no border, no watermark.
